description: Letter from Ismāʿīl b. Yosef b. Abī ʿUqba, probably in Palermo, to his maternal uncle Yosef Ibn ʿAwkal and to his brothers. Dating: ca. 1030 CE. Alludes to concerns about illegal substitution of flax: "I think there is no doubt that they substituted for the bale which you bought in the Qālūṣ (the flax market in Fustat) another bale which they packed instead.” (Goldberg, Trade and Institutions, 82.)
description_he: מכתב מישמאעיל בן יוסף בן אבי עוקבה, כנראה מפלרמו, לדודו יוסף אבן עוכאל ולאחיו, כ-1030. מתייחס לחשש מהחלפת פשתן בלתי חוקית בשוק פוסטאט.
